LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR EVIDENCE SANCTIONS, ISSUE SANCTIONS, TERMINATING SANCTIONS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF PAMELA SNYDER HELD ON SEP-25-2019, IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT ADOPTS THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S RECOMMENDATION WITH A COUPLE OF MODIFICATIONS. DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AS TO MONETARY SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 ORDER, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,665.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE WITH LEAVE TO RENEW TO SEEK TERMINATING EVIDENTIARY OR ISSUE SANCTIONS IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RESPOND TO THE WRITTEN DISCOVERY BY OCTOBER 15, 2019. HOWEVER IF THE 10/21/19 TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED BY MORE THAN TWO MONTHS, PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN RESPONSES WILL BE 45 DAYS FROM THE DATE DEFENDANT SERVES ITS NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER, THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES SHALL BE STRICKEN AS AN APPROPRIATE SANCTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Randy Moss, Esq. 415-949-1900 of Demler Armstrong & Rowland for Defendant FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court summarizes the background of the case and shares its thoughts. Both sides argue and Ms. Snyder addresses the Court. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctins, Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pamela Snyder.

The Court adopts the Judge Pro Tem's recommendation with a couple of modifications. Defendant Fire Insurance Exchange's motion is granted in part as to monetary sanctions pursuant to the Court's September 9, 2019 order, in the amount of $1,665.00 to be paid within 10 days of this order. The motion is denied without prejudice with leave to renew to seek terminating evidentiary or issue sanctions if Plaintiff fails to respond to the written discovery by October 15, 2019. However if the 10/21/19 trial date is continued by more than two months, Plaintiff's written responses will be 45 days from the date Defendant serves its notice of entry of the order continuing the trial date.If Plaintiff fails to comply with this order, the request for punitive damages shall be stricken as an appropriate sanction.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
